mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration that triggers algorithmic risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 20-day horizons or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by 0.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization peaks that precede inventory corrections and generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 70% of late-cycle periods despite rising industrial production indices.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances that suppress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 20 days in 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending confidence and triggers positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows zero predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample 5-day return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.4 million units signals a construction sector contraction that reduces downstream capital goods demand and drives negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns exceed 0% in 60% of months where housing starts drop below 1.4 million.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indices reflects robust manufacturing output that directly increases corporate profit margins and generates positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between industrial production growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 across all tested periods.
